params.name: The Rhind Papyrus
params.description:
On Oct 3, 1885 AD, British archaeologist Rhind purchased a batch of papyri on the banks of the Nile in a small town.
The Rhind Mathematical Papyrus is a long, rectangular sheet of paper written about 1,000 years before the Common Era and is the oldest mathematical book in the world. It is about five meters long and thirty-three centimeters wide, covered with dense hieroglyphic writing, and contains the solution methods for more than eighty mathematical problems. The arithmetic section records …
